response.justification: The candidate is a broadly experienced, craftsmanship-oriented engineer with genuine language-tooling interest (D test runners, assertion libraries, a stint on the LabWindows/CVI IDE at National Instruments), but the core stack for this role is largely absent: there is no substantive Java/Kotlin production experience, no Spring or enterprise-framework background, and no deep JVM work. His recent decade centers on TypeScript/Node and DLang. The IDE-internals spark is real but too thin against the hard requirements of enterprise JVM tooling.
strengths: Direct IDE development experience (NI LabWindows/CVI) plus authored developer tooling (Trial test runner, Fluent-Asserts)
Strong software craftsmanship practices: TDD, CI/CD, pairing, trunk-based development, plus teaching/workshop experience
Proven ability to work on complex, high-scale systems (millions of requests/sec at Dunnhumby, low-latency async I/O)
concerns: No meaningful Java/Kotlin depth — Java appears only peripherally, and Kotlin not at all
No Spring, Quarkus, Hibernate, or enterprise JVM framework experience
Recent experience (TypeScript/Node full-stack, AI tooling) is far from JVM internals and application-server integration
